title: Show and persist the button click counter

requirements.summary: Show the current click count in the main UI while the red button is visible.
acceptanceCriteria: The UI shows a numeric click counter on the main screen while the button is visible (before the explosion).
The counter increments by 1 on each successful button click (and does not increment during the explosion state).
The explosion trigger at exactly 1000 clicks continues to work as before.

file_operations.description: Render a click counter in the main UI near the existing header/button and ensure it reflects the current click state without changing the existing explosion sequence behavior (still triggers at exactly 1000, same animation + redirect timing).

requirements.summary: Persist the click count to browser storage so refresh restores progress toward 1000 clicks.
acceptanceCriteria: After a page refresh, the displayed click counter restores to the last saved value.
The persisted counter updates after each click (no more than one click behind).
If stored data is missing or invalid, the counter safely falls back to 0 without breaking the UI.

file_operations.description: Add a small React hook that reads an initial numeric value from localStorage (with validation and safe fallback to 0), exposes the value/setter, and writes updates back to localStorage whenever the value changes.

Adopt the persisted number hook for clickCount so the initial value restores from localStorage, updates are saved after each click, and invalid/missing stored values fall back to 0 while keeping the existing explosion trigger and redirect behavior intact.
